agent_output: {"summary":{"verdict":"BLOCK","one_liner":"IDOR vulnerability on authenticated route and unprotected location endpoint expose all delivery data to any caller"},"critical_findings":[{"title":"Broken Object-Level Authorization (IDOR) on /deliveries/:id","risk":"Any authenticated user can read any delivery by guessing or enumerating IDs — no check that req.user owns the delivery","severity":"Critical","location":"delivery-routes.ts:4-6 (GET /deliveries/:id handler)","reproduction":"1. Authenticate as User A. 2. GET /deliveries/<User B's delivery ID>. 3. Receive full delivery payload for a delivery you don't own.","fix":"Add authorization check: `if (delivery.userId !== req.user.id) return res.status(403).json({ error: 'Forbidden' });` — or use a scoped query: `db.deliveries.findByIdAndUser(req.params.id, req.user.id)`","time_to_fix":"15 minutes","acceptance_criteria_affected":"Data isolation between customers"},{"title":"Unauthenticated location endpoint leaks real-time position data for any delivery","risk":"The /deliveries/:id/location endpoint is completely public. An attacker can enumerate delivery IDs and track every active driver in real time — a physical-safety and competitive-intelligence risk.","severity":"Critical","location":"delivery-routes.ts:8-11 (GET /deliveries/:id/location handler)","reproduction":"1. No auth needed. 2. Enumerate GET /deliveries/1/location, /deliveries/2/location, etc. 3. Collect real-time GPS for every delivery in the system.","fix":"At minimum, require a per-delivery tracking token (e.g. /track/:trackingToken/location) that is unguessable, or add rate-limiting plus requireAuth. Do NOT rely on sequential integer IDs as the only gate.","time_to_fix":"30 minutes","acceptance_criteria_affected":"Driver safety, customer privacy, GDPR location-data requirements"},{"title":"Server crash when JWT_SECRET env var is unset","risk":"process.env.JWT_SECRET! uses a non-null assertion. If the env var is missing (common in misconfigured deploys, new environments, or CI), jwt.verify receives undefined as the secret. Depending on the jwt library version this either throws a cryptic error inside the try-catch (silently rejecting all tokens — full outage for authenticated routes) or, worse, some libraries treat an empty/undefined secret as no verification, accepting any token.","severity":"High","location":"auth-middleware.ts:7","reproduction":"1. Start the service without JWT_SECRET in env. 2. Send any request with a Bearer token. 3. Observe either all requests rejected (outage) or all tokens accepted (auth bypass).","fix":"Validate JWT_SECRET at startup: `if (!process.env.JWT_SECRET) throw new Error('JWT_SECRET is required');` — fail fast, not at request time.","time_to_fix":"5 minutes","acceptance_criteria_affected":"Service availability and authentication integrity"}],"important_issues":[{"title":"No null check on delivery lookup","description":"findById returns null/undefined when the ID doesn't exist. The handler blindly calls res.json(delivery), sending a 200 with null body instead of 404. Same issue on getLocation.","location":"delivery-routes.ts lines 5 and 10"},{"title":"No input validation on :id parameter","description":"req.params.id is passed directly to DB queries with no type or format validation. If IDs are expected to be UUIDs or integers, malformed input could cause unexpected DB errors or injection depending on the ORM.","location":"delivery-routes.ts lines 5 and 10"}],"suggestions":["Add rate limiting to the public location endpoint to slow enumeration even after the auth fix","Use UUIDs instead of sequential IDs for deliveries to reduce enumerability","Add request logging/audit trail on delivery access for compliance"],"production_readiness":{"deployment_risks":"The IDOR and public location endpoint are exploitable immediately in production — this is not a future risk, it is a current one","rollback_capability":"No schema changes, rollback is safe if these routes were previously protected","backward_compatibility":"Adding auth to /location is a breaking change for any client currently consuming it without credentials","monitoring_coverage":"No mention of logging or monitoring on access patterns — enumeration attacks would go undetected","breaking_changes":{"api_breaking":true,"db_destructive":false,"shared_library":false,"auth_security":true,"data_pipeline":false,"config_changes":false,"details":["Adding auth or tracking-token requirement to /location is breaking for current public consumers","Adding authorization to /deliveries/:id will return 403 where it previously returned 200 — correct but breaking for any client relying on cross-user access"]},"safe_to_deploy":false,"rollback_plan":"Revert route file to previous version; no DB migration involved"},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":95,"high_confidence_areas":["IDOR is definitively present — no ownership check exists in the code","Location endpoint is definitively unauthenticated — no middleware attached","JWT_SECRET crash path is deterministic given missing env var"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact jwt library behavior with undefined secret depends on library and version","DB query injection risk depends on ORM sanitization we cannot see"],"assumptions":["deliveries have an owner/userId field","IDs are sequential or guessable rather than cryptographic UUIDs"]}}
